label: Day 1 — Afternoon: Name Games & Icebreakers
description: The first real programme session. At this point participants have eaten together and had time to settle — now we break the ice properly. The goal is simple: learn each other's names, laugh together, and begin to feel like a group rather than four separate national delegations.
objectives: Learn the names of all 32 participants through active play
Break national clusters and create first cross-cultural connections
Establish a light, playful group energy that will carry into Day 2
Generate the first shared memory of the exchange

steps.title: Circle of names
steps.body: Standing in a circle: each person says their name + one gesture. The group repeats both. Three rounds, increasing speed.

steps.title: Human Bingo
steps.body: Each participant gets a bingo card with prompts. First to complete a row wins — but everyone keeps going until time is up. Use the interactive bingo below.

steps.title: Name & superpower
steps.body: In groups of 6 (mixed nationality): each person introduces themselves with their name + one green superpower they wish they had. Groups then present the most interesting superpowers to the room.

steps.title: The name web
steps.body: Standing in a circle with a ball of wool. Each person says a name they remember, throws the wool to that person — keeping hold of their thread. The result is a web connecting the whole group.

steps.title: Closing reflection
steps.body: Brief facilitated debrief: one name you'll definitely remember tomorrow, and why. Then announce tomorrow's theme.

facilitatorNote: Energy is everything in this session. Move fast between activities, keep music playing between rounds. If the group is shy, model the energy yourself — be the first to do something ridiculous. Mixed-nationality groupings are non-negotiable from the very first moment.
